description: Input is cleaned after the prompt is assembled, leaving a window where raw input already shaped the prompt structure.
body: Failure mode: the pipeline assembles the prompt from raw input and then runs sanitization on the combined string, so raw delimiters or instructions have already altered the structure and may slip past a post-hoc clean. Detection signals: scrubbing operates on the assembled prompt, not the inputs; redaction and normalization run last; breakout payloads survive. Do not sanitize after assembly. Safer fallback: scrub, bound, and redact each untrusted input at the boundary before it enters the template, label it as data, and verify a delimiter-breakout payload cannot escape its region.
